corpus_note: Assertions grade outputs for pi-mono path citations and protocol quotes. Checked-in baselines: evals/graded-examples/<eval_id>/. Live runs: pi-skills-workspace/iteration-N/<eval_id>/.

evals.prompt: I have the same skill name in ~/.pi/agent/skills/foo and in my repo's .pi/skills/foo. Which file wins when pi loads skills, and where is that decided in pi-mono? List the exact source files and the merge order (CLI vs project vs package).
evals.expected_output: Answer cites resourcePrecedenceRank in package-manager.ts, mergePaths/load order in resource-loader.ts, first-name-wins in skills.ts; no invented behavior.

evals.prompt: Why would <available_skills> not appear in my system prompt when running pi with a custom tool set? Point to the exact condition in pi-mono source.
evals.expected_output: Cites buildSystemPrompt hasRead gate in system-prompt.ts.

evals.prompt: What happens when I send /skill:my-skill extra args in the pi editor? Which function expands it and where is the skill body read from disk?
evals.expected_output: Cites _expandSkillCommand and readFileSync in agent-session.ts with path to file.

evals.prompt: When does pi auto-compact, how does it choose where to cut, and can I tune it via settings?
evals.expected_output: Cites compaction.md: reserveTokens triggers, keepRecentTokens budget, cut-point avoids splitting tool results.

evals.prompt: How do I add a local Ollama model to pi so it appears in /model, and what compat flags might I need?
evals.expected_output: Cites models.md: minimal models.json entry with baseUrl, compat flags for non-standard servers.

evals.prompt: My extension calls extendResources with extra skillPaths. If a skill name collides with one already loaded earlier in reload(), which wins? Quote the merge/ordering logic from pi-mono.
evals.expected_output: extendResources appends paths; mergePaths preserves first resolved path; loadSkills first name wins — cite resource-loader.ts and skills.ts.

evals.prompt: Where is the primary first-party documentation for authoring Pi extensions, and where is the examples index on disk in pi-mono?
evals.expected_output: Points to pi-mono/packages/coding-agent/docs/extensions.md and examples path under packages/coding-agent/examples/extensions/.

evals.prompt: When two loaded extensions register conflicting tools or commands, what does pi-mono coding-agent do — drop one extension or keep both and report diagnostics?
evals.expected_output: Cites detectExtensionConflicts / errors pushed to extensionsResult in resource-loader reload path; keep all loaded, surface diagnostics.

evals.prompt: In pi package resolution, what numeric rank does origin package get in resourcePrecedenceRank, and what does lower rank mean for sort order?
evals.expected_output: package returns 4; lower rank sorts earlier in resolved lists — cite package-manager.ts comment block.

evals.prompt: Which pi-mono markdown file documents pi install / pi list / package.json pi manifest for Pi packages?
evals.expected_output: pi-mono/packages/coding-agent/docs/packages.md (and cross-ref README Pi Packages).

evals.prompt: Where does the end-user-facing Pi Packages section (pi install examples) live in pi-mono?
evals.expected_output: packages/coding-agent/README.md Pi Packages section.

evals.prompt: I need to add a new LLM provider to pi-ai. What is the ordered file checklist in pi-mono AGENTS.md for packages/ai (types, provider file, register-builtins, tests, coding-agent touchpoints)?
evals.expected_output: Lists sections from pi-mono/AGENTS.md Adding a New LLM Provider without inventing extra steps.

evals.prompt: Where is the canonical public API overview for stream/complete, Context, and Tool definitions in pi-mono?
evals.expected_output: packages/ai/README.md.

evals.prompt: After README, where should I open implementation files for a specific provider under pi-ai?
evals.expected_output: pi-mono/packages/ai/src/providers/ and related files per AGENTS.md checklist.

evals.prompt: Summarize the prompt() event sequence for the Agent class (high level) and point to the diagram or list in pi-mono that defines it.
evals.expected_output: Uses packages/agent/README.md event flow, not invented ordering.

evals.prompt: Where are the numbered SDK examples for createAgentSession and skills configuration in pi-mono?
evals.expected_output: packages/coding-agent/examples/sdk/ with README index.

evals.prompt: Which markdown doc in coding-agent describes SDK integration at a high level?
evals.expected_output: docs/sdk.md.

evals.prompt: From pi-web-ui README only: what stores and backend class set up IndexedDB for settings and sessions in the Quick Start, and what CSS import is required?
evals.expected_output: Matches README snippet classes (SettingsStore, SessionsStore, IndexedDBStorageBackend, app.css path).

evals.prompt: Where is the minimal Vite example app for pi-web-ui in pi-mono?
evals.expected_output: packages/web-ui/example/

evals.prompt: Which two pi-mono packages does web-ui README list as peers alongside @mariozechner/pi-web-ui for a typical chat setup?
evals.expected_output: pi-agent-core and pi-ai per README install line.

evals.prompt: Why is Node readline unsafe for pi RPC JSONL framing, and what characters cause the problem? Answer from pi-mono docs only.
evals.expected_output: Quotes rpc.md: U+2028 and U+2029 inside JSON strings; readline splits on those.

evals.prompt: For a Node service embedding pi, what does rpc.md recommend instead of subprocess RPC, and which source file does it point TypeScript users to?
evals.expected_output: Prefer AgentSession; cites agent-session.ts path as in rpc.md intro.

evals.prompt: What events does pi --mode json emit and how does it differ from --mode rpc? Show a jq example.
evals.expected_output: Cites json.md: --mode json is read-only stdout events; --mode rpc is bidirectional.

evals.prompt: How does the Pi TUI update the terminal screen without flickering? Reference the specific sequence or CSI code used.
evals.expected_output: Mentions differential rendering and CSI 2026 synchronized output.

evals.prompt: How do I create a custom component in pi-tui that respects width constraints and handles keyboard input? Point me to the interface and the key detection utility.
evals.expected_output: Cites Component interface with render(width), matchesKey for input.

evals.prompt: How do I show a modal overlay dialog in pi-tui, and what options control its positioning?
evals.expected_output: Cites showOverlay from tui README and at least one positioning option such as anchor, row/col, or margin.

evals.prompt: What are the two files Mom uses to manage conversation history per channel, and which one is sent to the LLM?
evals.expected_output: log.jsonl is the source of truth, context.jsonl is sent to the LLM.

evals.prompt: Should I run Mom in Docker or host mode, and what are the security tradeoffs?
evals.expected_output: Cites sandbox.md: host mode is the default, Docker mode is the recommended isolated option, and explains the security tradeoffs.

evals.prompt: How do I schedule a periodic task for Mom to run every morning in a specific channel?
evals.expected_output: Cites events.md: periodic event JSON in `/workspace/events/`, equivalent to host `data/events/` when the data dir is mounted into the sandbox.

evals.prompt: How do I configure pi pods with a DataCrunch NFS volume?
evals.expected_output: Mentions pi pods setup with --mount.

evals.prompt: How do I start a Qwen model on a multi-GPU pod and what tool-call parser does pi use for it?
evals.expected_output: Cites pods README: pi start with hermes parser for Qwen.

evals.prompt: How do I pass custom vLLM arguments like tensor-parallel-size when the predefined model CLI shortcuts aren't enough?
evals.expected_output: Cites pods README: --vllm flag passes raw args.

evals.prompt: How do I create a custom theme in pi coding agent?
evals.expected_output: Copy dark.json, edit color values, and use pi --theme.

evals.prompt: How do I remap the submit key in pi from Enter to Ctrl+Enter, and what namespace format do keybinding IDs use?
evals.expected_output: Cites keybindings.md: tui.* namespaced IDs in ~/.pi/agent/keybindings.json.

evals.prompt: Where are prompt templates loaded from, what argument syntax do they use, and is there a documented include directive?
evals.expected_output: Cites prompt-templates.md: locations, frontmatter, /name invocation, positional args, and that there is no documented include directive.
